compositional_truth_check.P4_Black_Pot: Present (Shared with P3, single leaf specimen)

inventory_reconciliation.notes: P1 and P2 are devoid of visible botanical specimens. P3 and P4 are the only active biological units.

plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen remains stable; white rabbit anchor present.
plant_audit.P4: Single leaf specimen remains, showing signs of chlorosis or light-stress.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, moist, consistent across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P4/P3 area, likely used as calcium amendment or mulch.
biome_observations.anomalies: None detected beyond the absence of P1/P2 occupants.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: P1 and P2 have transitioned from potentially occupied to completely bare soil. P3 and P4 have remained static in position and structure.

anomalies: Total loss of P1 and P2 specimens
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of decline. While the Pothos (P3) and the associated specimen in P4 remain, the yellow pot (P1) and the black pot (P2) have suffered total systemic loss of their original occupants. The environment appears to be maintained with consistent moisture, but the lack of growth in P1 and P2 suggests either recent removal or total plant mortality. The rabbit anchor remains a constant in the P3/P4 cluster.
